title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
During an investigation into the disappearance of Tom Brad, Jonah Lincoln provides an account of his activities. Lincoln states that he met Mrs. Brad and Helen Brad for dinner in Longchamps and later took them to the Park Theatre. He claims he returned to his office to work on accounts with an auditor, though he later clarifies that the auditor left at eight o'clock, leaving him alone. Lincoln and the Brad women took the Long Island train and a taxi home, arriving around one o'clock. Lincoln notes they did not notice anything unusual, specifically mentioning they did not look at the path leading to the gazebo because it was dark. Mrs. Brad explains she only discovered Tom was missing in the morning when Fox woke them.

The inquiry shifts to personal histories and connections. Lincoln reveals he has an unnamed sister, Hester, who resides on Oyster Island, where a man named Harleot has established a "Sun Cult" involving nudism. Mrs. Brad discloses that Tom was Romanian and had a previous wife in Europe. The discussion turns to Meagal, a Greek amateur explorer who has been sailing on his steam yacht, the *Helen*, for almost a year. Helen Brad suggests Meagal went to the South Pacific. Lincoln notes that Brad and Meagal have been in the carpet import business together for sixteen years and are financially stable, suggesting money is not the motive for the crime. When Ellery questions if religion is a factor, Lincoln points to the nature of the crime—nailing someone to a cross. Mrs. Brad adds that while Tom was baptized in a Greek Orthodox church, he was considered an atheist who did not believe in rituals.
